notes: No pre-existing GB200 MiniMax M3 config, run, log, artifact, or metric; perf-changelog.yaml untouched.
Active research resumed after explicit GO; three-minute heartbeat started.

decision: Use reciprocal p90 TPOT for the required tail-interactivity frontier; stored p90_intvty is not equivalent because reciprocal reverses percentile order.

checks: join drain and no active-join leakage
notes: No runtime claim yet; first TP4 1P/1D c1 smoke remains required.
No node excluded; cluster fully idle before first dispatch.
DAG fanout can transiently make wire in-flight exceed one while the single root lane remains bounded; no runaway session concurrency observed.

decision: Probe native MultiConnector with NixlConnector first and SimpleCPUOffloadConnector second; never exceed 192 GiB per worker on this allocation.
Use 1/p90_tpot for the required p90-tail interactivity frontier and report p90_intvty only as a schema caveat.

notes: All accelerated-warmup wire requests returned before profiling; paused DAG work persisted, including one rootless lane holding a lane credit. This is the required no-leak drain/handoff behavior.
All 27 exported records are profiling-only. Peak root-session slots equaled target concurrency 1; wire fanout peaked at 3 without session leakage.

cause: MiniMax shared-FS INFMAX_WORKSPACE retained aggregate and checkpoint JSON outside GITHUB_WORKSPACE, so the workflow found 0/1 expected results after Slurm completed successfully.
fix: Sync matching aggregate and checkpoint files from INFMAX_WORKSPACE back to GITHUB_WORKSPACE in launch_gb200-nv.sh before workflow validation.

purpose: Logarithmic topology and concurrency search; validate shared-workspace artifact fix.

notes: All three two-node jobs started immediately on distinct nodes.

cause: All DP4/EP4 ranks fail engine initialization with ValueError: No MXFP8 MoE backends available.
decision: Exclude TP1+DP-attention+EP for MiniMax M3 MXFP8 on vLLM 04c2a8de; retain both nodes because evidence is a deterministic kernel/topology incompatibility, not node failure.

notes: Directional only because c1 completed 6-7 long-context requests in 300 seconds. Both topologies remain non-dominated across the two required objectives pending higher-concurrency points and final 900-second confirmation.

pareto_observation: TEP4 dominates TP4 for throughput versus p90 E2E at c4; both remain non-dominated for throughput versus required tail interactivity because TP4 trades 1.3% throughput for 22.1% higher interactivity.
cooldown_observation: The intentionally short 300-second probe reached the scenario cutoff with active DAG joins. Final candidates require >=900 seconds; cancelled records are excluded from metrics.

cause: After all 94 TEP4 c4 credits returned, cancellation drain still waited on an unset event and logged a false timeout with Need 0 more credits.
fix: Set all_credits_returned_event immediately when outstanding credits are zero after cancel_all_credits.
validation: 46 phase-runner unit tests and Ruff passed.

cause: JSONL shutdown waited on the periodic background task together with finite flush tasks, causing a false Timeout waiting for 0 pending flush tasks warning and risking cancellation of unrelated tasks.
fix: Track finite flush tasks separately and wait only for them before the final synchronous buffer flush.
validation: 54 targeted unit tests and Ruff passed.

pareto_observation: TP4 dominates TEP4 at c8 across throughput, p90 E2E, and required tail interactivity. The low GPU-cache hit makes TP4 c8 the selected CPU-offload stress point.

c12 is beyond the throughput/E2E knee: severe prefill queuing collapses throughput and drives p90 TTFT above 113 seconds. TP4 c12 remains a candidate only for the separately defined throughput-versus-1/p90-TPOT frontier.

memory_rationale: Measured prefill baseline is 460 GiB against 840 GiB allocatable host DRAM. The 192 GiB ceiling leaves at least 188 GiB allocation headroom; swap is zero. MultiConnector orders NixlConnector before lazy SimpleCPUOffloadConnector so disaggregated transfer remains primary.
guardrail: Launcher rejects any CPU_OFFLOAD_GIB outside 64, 128, or 192.

cause: vLLM MultiConnector and SimpleCPUOffloadConnector initialize correctly, but Dynamo 1.3.0.dev20260618 rejects top-level kv_connector=MultiConnector in PD request handling; its registry supports only NixlConnector and MooncakeConnector.
fix: Patch Dynamo's container-local PD protocol registry during setup so MultiConnector uses NixlConnectorProtocol. This preserves NIXL as the first MultiConnector child while SimpleCPUOffloadConnector remains a local fallback tier.

finding: srt runs the recipe setup script before installing the pinned Dynamo wheel, so the initial bridge location was too early and was not rerun.
fix: Patch srtctl runtime_scripts/dynamo_wheels.py in the staged clone so it updates Dynamo's installed protocol registry immediately after wheel installation and before the worker process starts.
validation: Applied the exact launcher-generated transform to the pinned de59739b runtime script; the transformed installer compiles and the generated 64 GiB recipe contains the expected MultiConnector configuration.
No BranchOrchestrator leak warning, no zero-credit drain timeout, no pending-flush timeout; aggregate and 300 profiling-only records exported.
